items.title: Counting a billion people: Inside India's mega census drive
items.summary: SUKHWINDER Kaur battles the scorching sun as she trudges door-to-door in New Delhi as part of India's colossal exercise to count more than one billion citizens. Kaur is among three million census enumerators fanning out across cities and villages in an exercise that will help shape policy for the country's estimated 1.4 billion people for years to come. "I went back home, but returned again in the evening," said Kaur, who will be paid 25,000 rupees (US$270) for the assignment, like other census enumerators.

items.title: 146 held in Terengganu anti-drug crackdown
items.summary: CHUKAI: The National Anti-Drugs Agency (AADK) detained 146 individuals, including four women, in a five-day operation carried out across Terengganu from Saturday. State AADK director Arzmi Abdullah said the arrests were made in Kemaman, Setiu and Marang, involving individuals aged between 18 and 61. Those detained comprised 142 men and four women, including civil servants, labourers, restaurant workers and a single mother.

items.title: PetChem expects continued market uncertainty, supply chain volatility in 2026
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Petronas Chemicals Group Bhd (PetChem) is heading into 2026 expecting continued market uncertainty and supply chain volatility, driven in part by uneven global recovery and recent geopolitical developments in Middle East affecting feedstock flows. PetChem said it will focus on operational discipline and tighter capital management as it navigates a more challenging environment, while selectively advancing initiatives to strengthen portfolio resilience. At the AGM, PetChem said its financial and operational performance for the year ended Dec 31, 2025 reflected disciplined execution amid global headwinds in the chemicals market.

items.title: KLIA baggage disruption sparks transport security review, weekly reports ordered - Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A five-hour disruption to the baggage handling system at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) caused by a power outage has prompted tighter oversight on transport operational security, with Malaysia Airports Holdings Bhd (MAHB) ordered to submit weekly progress reports to the Transport Ministry. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the incident, which affected baggage services at KLIA, lasted about five hours before being fully restored at 10.30pm. Fahmi said MAHB will implement immediate mitigation measures, including increasing manpower at arrival halls and other identified critical areas to reduce the risk of similar operational breakdowns.

items.title: Govt WFH in its first week saves 334,000 litres of fuel and RM678,000, says Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Malaysia’s work-from-home push for civil servants has saved about RM678,000 in fuel in just over a week, Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said today. He said the initiative, which began on April 15, has already involved some 155,000 civil servants nationwide. “Since April 25, he said the initiative has resulted in an estimated fuel saving of 334,000 litres, equivalent to approximately RM678,000,” he said.

items.title: Global energy crisis may normalise faster than Covid-19 impact: Affin chief economist
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The global energy shock triggered by the Middle East conflict is expected to normalise faster than the prolonged economic downturn seen during the Covid-19 pandemic. However, Affin Bank chief economist Alan Tan Chew Leong said the outlook remains highly dependent on how long the conflict persists. This remains uncertain," said Tan.

items.title: Senior citizen, Indonesian single mother fined for wrongful confinement of migrants
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: A senior citizen and an Indonesian single mother were fined RM3,000 each by the magistrate's court here today for the wrongful confinement of two migrants and the illegal possession of their passports. The duo were charged under Section 340 of the Penal Code for wrongful confinement, punishable under Section 342 and read together with Section 34 of the same Code for common intention. For the first charge, the court fined the duo RM2,000 each.

items.title: Indonesian woman cries for her boyfriend after being charged with prostitution
items.summary: BATU PAHAT: An Indonesian woman broke down in tears while searching for her boyfriend after being charged in the magistrate's court today with prostitution and abusing her social visit pass. Waryanti, 41, pleaded not guilty to the charges before magistrate Arun Noval Dass. Her compatriot, Wayan Lawungwati, 38, who was charged separately, pleaded guilty.

items.title: One night, one month: Syed Saddiq says fiancée Bella Astillah out-earns his MP salary per show
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman has revealed that his fiancée, singer-actress Bella Astillah, can earn in a single performance what he makes in a month as a politician, Berita Harian reported today. Speaking in a podcast interview, he said the comparison highlights the stark differences in income structures between the entertainment industry and politics. Addressing speculation that he might stop Bella from continuing her career after marriage, Syed Saddiq dismissed the idea.

items.title: MCMC opens 65 investigations over 463 fake news takedown requests - Fahmi
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) has received 463 requests to remove online content linked to fake news surrounding global supply chain issues, with authorities opening 65 investigation papers. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the cases primarily involved misinformation related to fuel prices and developments surrounding the Strait of Hormuz. He said of the total cases, enforcement action has already been initiated.

items.title: Chief Secretary tasked with studying private sector WFH savings to assess national impact
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Chief Secretary to the Government Tan Sri Shamsul Azri Abu Bakar has been tasked with gathering data from the private sector on work-from-home (WFH) practices to assess the broader national impact of remote working, including potential fuel and operational cost savings. He said the government had analysed savings generated through civil service WFH arrangements and wants to study the assessment on private sector participation. The directive comes as the public sector has recorded fuel savings of 334,000 litres, worth RM678,000, since mid-April through expanded remote working arrangements.

items.title: Six bands advance to RTD 'Battle of the Bands' finals
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Six bands from various higher education institutions have secured their spots in the grand finals of the "Battle of the Bands" competition, an innovative road safety advocacy initiative by the Road Transport Department (RTD). Organised by the RTD, the programme is designed to use music as a creative medium to deliver road safety messages to the public. The six finalists vying for the top prize are Nada Stereo from Universiti Pendidikan Sultan Idris; Tralala from Universiti Malaya; Scarla from Universiti Utara Malaysia; Putra Simponi from Universiti Putra Malaysia; Difficulties from Akademi Seni Budaya dan Warisan Kebangsaan; and Minit Terakhir from Universiti Sains Malaysia.

items.title: Extreme heat threatens global food systems, UN agencies warn
items.summary: ROME: Extreme heat is pushing global agrifood systems to the brink, threatening the livelihoods and health of more than a billion people, according to a new report by the UN's food and weather agencies. The United Nations Food and Agriculture Organisation (FAO) and the World Meteorological Organisation (WMO) said heatwaves are becoming more frequent, intense and prolonged, damaging crops, livestock, fisheries and forests. In some cases it is even dictating if they can still work," said Kaveh Zahedi, head of FAO's climate change office.

items.title: Malaysia pivots to asymmetric warfare with focus on missiles, drones
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia is recalibrating its defence strategy towards asymmetric warfare, with a stronger emphasis on missiles, drones and network-centric systems to address evolving security challenges. Defence Minister Datuk Seri Mohamed Khaled Nordin said recent procurements have focused on enhancing naval combat capabilities, particularly for assets operating in strategic waters. He said the acquisitions include surface-to-surface and surface-to-air missile systems for Littoral Combat Ships (LCS) and Littoral Mission Ships (LMS), aimed at strengthening operational readiness in key areas such as the Straits of Malacca, South China Sea, Sulu Sea and Sulawesi Sea.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz to officiate Negri Sembilan assembly sitting tomorrow
items.summary: SEREMBAN: Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, is scheduled to officiate the opening of the first meeting of the fourth session of the 15th State Legislative Assembly tomorrow. Following tomorrow's ceremony, the assembly sitting will continue from April 27 to 29. Customarily, the ceremony is attended by the four Undang: Datuk Mubarak Dohak (Sungei Ujong), Datuk Maarof Mat Rashad (Jelebu), Datuk Muhammad Abdullah (Johol), and Datuk Abdul Rahim Yasin (Rembau), as well as Tunku Besar Tampin Tunku Syed Razman Tunku Syed Idrus Al Qadri.

items.title: KLIA baggage fiasco: MAHB to boost manpower, submit weekly reports after Cabinet review
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Malaysia Airports Holdings Berhad (MAHB) has been directed to take urgent measures, including boosting manpower at critical passenger areas, following a major baggage handling disruption at Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) last weekend. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the matter was raised by Transport Minister Anthony Loke during today’s Cabinet meeting, where concerns were voiced over the operational failure. However, the system failure resulted in 1,061 bags being short-shipped (left behind) and 120 bags recorded as missing upon arrival, causing significant inconvenience to travellers.

items.title: Perlis mufti denies links to misappropriation of zakat funds
items.summary: Asri said zakat administration in the state is managed by the Perlis Islamic Religious and Malay Customs Council (MAIPs) rather than the mufti's office. Asri said zakat contributors in Perlis can propose recipients, such as non-governmental organisations (NGOs) or religious institutions. The funds go directly to the recipient," he said.

items.title: #NSTviral: 'Tan Sri or Tauke's brother? Doesn't matter- pay for your car wash!' says sign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: A notice at a car wash reminding customers to "pay up" once services are rendered has caught the attention of Redditors, with the sign saying that it "doesn't care who you are." "Beginning 2025, it doesn't matter whether you are a Tan Sri, Datuk Seri, Datuk, a cop, a gangster, a best friend, or the tauke's (business owner) brother… Pay up now! Business is business, pay up and no drama," wrote one Redditor, Alexisreddit516.

items.title: Takeovers not a crime, SC says amid ‘corporate mafia’ allegations
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Securities Commission Malaysia (SC) today said it has no authority to act on allegations of a “corporate mafia” if the issues fall outside securities laws. Chairman Datuk Mohammad Faiz Azmi said the regulator’s role is limited to market and securities offences, not criminal conduct such as intimidation or extortion, business news outlet The Edge reported. Faiz said that corporate activities like share purchases or takeover bids are legal as long as they comply with established takeover regulations.

items.title: Melaka police track scammers using department's name to solicit donations
items.summary: MELAKA: Police are tracking down individuals who used the force's name to solicit donations from the public through WhatsApp. Melaka police chief Datuk Dzulkhairi Mukhtar said a report was lodged regarding a viral message claiming that the Melaka contingent's Traffic Enforcement and Investigation Department was organising an integrity and driving course. The message included photos of uniformed officers and locations within the Melaka police headquarters to appear authentic.

items.title: Tuanku Muhriz to officiate Negri Sembilan assembly sitting tomorrow amid Undang controversy
items.summary: SEREMBAN, April 22 — The Yang di-Pertuan Besar of Negeri Sembilan, Tuanku Muhriz Tuanku Munawir, is due to officiate the opening of the State Legislative Assembly tomorrow, with the ceremony proceeding as planned despite ongoing scrutiny of the state’s royal institution. State Secretary Datuk Mohd Zafir Ibrahim confirmed today that the opening will take place at 10 am, with Tuanku Muhriz inspecting the guard of honour and delivering the royal address, Berita Harian reported. “Tomorrow’s DUN opening ceremony will proceed as scheduled.

items.title: Lawyer urges fair probe in RM230mil zakat fund case
items.summary: SHAH ALAM: The lawyer for a non-governmental organisation (NGO) deputy chairman, who has been remanded over an alleged RM230 million zakat fund misappropriation case, has called for space for investigations to proceed fairly. Mohd Radzlan Jalaludin said the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) investigation into his client has so far been conducted properly and professionally. We should give MACC the space to carry out its investigation fairly," he told reporters outside the magistrate's court here today.

items.title: KJ: Malaysia could have passed tobacco 'generational endgame' law
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Malaysia could have introduced tobacco "generational endgame" (GEG) legislation similar to the United Kingdom's newly approved Tobacco and Vapes Bill if the cabinet had not been wrongly advised, former health minister Khairy Jamaluddin said. Khairy said he had prepared the legislation before leaving the ministry, but the provision was later removed. "Malaysia could have passed this law, but the provision was dropped after the tobacco and vape industry reportedly convinced the cabinet against it.

items.title: 200 million-litre diesel shipment to Australia not from Malaysia - Fahmi
items.summary: Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said checks with relevant authorities confirmed that the diesel in question was not Malaysian in origin. "I read a report from an Australian news agency mentioning 200 million litres of diesel involving several countries including Brunei and Malaysia. But it is not Malaysian diesel," he said.

items.title: King extends personal contribution to assist Sandakan fire victims
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has extended a personal contribution to assist families affected by a fire in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan. According to a Facebook post on Sultan Ibrahim's official page, the king decreed that the contribution will be channelled through Yayasan Sultan Ibrahim Johor (YSIJ) to help ease the burden of families impacted by the incident. Sultan Ibrahim has instructed the foundation to liaise with the Sabah state government to ensure the assistance reaches the victims.

items.title: Kelantan denies RM5.5bil solar project application, approval
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Kelantan state government clarifies that it has neither approved nor received any application related to a proposed RM5.5 billion solar and eco-development project in the state. In a statement, the state government said the information published in relation to the project is inaccurate and does not involve Kelantan. The state government is responding to reports regarding a company that had announced plans on April 7 to develop a RM5.5 billion project in northern Kelantan.

items.title: Selangor PAS chief labels Shah Alam floods a ‘planning failure’, demands action plan
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 22 — The Selangor government has been urged to increase green space allocation in Shah Alam residential areas to 30 per cent and review its development policies, with the state’s PAS chief labelling the recurring floods a “planning failure”. Halim Tamuri, debating the Royal Address at the Selangor State Legislative Assembly today, argued that the state capital’s flood woes reflect ingrained weaknesses in planning and management. “The failure to address flooding in Shah Alam is no longer a simple matter,” Halim said.

items.title: Thailand backs Myanmar's push to normalise Asean ties
items.summary: BANGKOK: Thailand wants to play a leading role in Myanmar's bid to normalise its ties with Southeast Asian bloc Asean after five years on the sidelines, the Thai foreign minister said ahead of a meeting on Wednesday with new President Min Aung Hlaing. "We want to support their return to Asean. Myanmar remains part of Asean has been represented at top-level meetings by ministerial officials.

items.title: Ukraine to restart Russian oil pipeline to Europe 'within a few hours'
items.summary: KYIV: Ukraine will resume shipping Russian oil to Hungary and Slovakia "within a few hours" Wednesday following the completion of repairs to the Druzhba pipeline damaged by a Russian attack, a Kyiv official told AFP. The pipeline has been at the centre of a standoff between Hungary and Slovakia, which still import Russian oil via the pipeline, and Ukraine and the European Union. Following Ukraine confirming the pipeline had been completed, Hungary's energy giant MOL requested that about 100,000 tonnes of oil be shipped through the pipeline to Hungary and Slovakia, the Ukrainian official said.

items.title: [UPDATED] Govt ramps up cost controls across ministries, agencies
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government has directed ministries, departments and agencies to step up cost-saving measures, including holding more meetings online, reducing overseas travel and prioritising the use of government premises for official programmes. Communications Minister Datuk Fahmi Fadzil said the reminder was issued by Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim during today's Cabinet meeting as part of broader efforts to optimise resource use and manage fuel consumption. "This includes conducting more meetings online to reduce face-to-face meetings, reducing programmes and overseas travel, and prioritising agency or government premises for workshops and training programmes," Fahmi said.

items.title: [UPDATED] WFH policy saves 334,000 litres of fuel worth RM678,000 since April 15
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The government's work-from-home (WFH) policy for civil servants has led to fuel savings of 334,000 litres, worth an estimated RM678,000, since it was started. The government has also announced wider government efforts to optimise resource use, including directives for ministries and agencies to reduce overseas travel, increase the use of virtual meetings, and prioritise government premises for official training and workshops. "This includes conducting more meetings online to reduce face-to-face meetings, cutting overseas programmes and travel, and prioritising agency or government premises for workshops and training programmes," Fahmi said.

items.title: Federal Court to hear Isa Samad's corruption case review in May
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Federal Court will hear on May 13 an application by former Felda chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Samad to review his conviction and sentence in a RM3 million corruption case linked to a hotel acquisition. Deputy public prosecutor Afzainizam Abdul Aziz and Isa's lawyer, Datuk Abu Bakar Isa Ramat, confirmed the hearing date. Isa, 77, has been serving a six-year jail sentence at the Sungai Buloh Prison since Feb 10 after the apex court reinstated the imprisonment and a RM15.45 million fine.

items.title: NASA unveils new space telescope to give 'atlas of the universe'
items.summary: WASHINGTON: NASA unveiled a new telescope on Tuesday to scan vast swathes of the universe for planets outside our solar system and probe the mysteries of dark matter and dark energy. The Roman space telescope is expected to discover tens of thousands of planets, possibly offering clarity about how many could be out there. "Roman will give the Earth a new atlas of the universe," NASA administrator Jared Isaacman told a news conference at the Goddard Space Flight Center in Maryland, where the telescope went on display.

items.title: China cautions of fragile peace as US extends Iran ceasefire
items.summary: BEIJING: China warned on Wednesday that the situation in the Middle East was at a "critical juncture" after US President Donald Trump extended a ceasefire to allow Iran more time to negotiate. Trump indefinitely pushed back the end of the two-week truce on Tuesday with Teheran yet to respond but he said a US blockade of Iran's ports would continue. "The current regional situation stands at a critical juncture transitioning between war and peace; the paramount priority remains to make every effort to prevent a resumption of hostilities," Chinese foreign ministry spokesman Guo Jiakun told a news briefing.

items.title: Duo fined RM6,000 for confining Indonesian migrants, withholding passports
items.summary: BATU PAHAT, April 22 — A 71-year-old man and a 43-year-old Indonesian single mother have each been fined RM3,000 after pleading guilty to unlawfully confining two Indonesian migrants and illegally retaining their passports. Ker Peng Hoe and Hartati Agostoni were charged before Magistrate Arun Noval Dass under Section 340 of the Penal Code, punishable under Section 342 read together with Section 34 of the same code, Berita Harian reported. The pair were accused of wrongfully confining two Indonesian women at a shophouse in Taman Flora Utama here between 9 am and 8.30 pm on April 19.

items.title: Erosion fears grow after another Casuarina tree collapses in Batu Ferringhi
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN: The collapse of another Casuarina tree along the Batu Ferringhi coastline here has heightened safety concerns. Nearby beach workers have warned of increasing risks linked to coastal erosion and unstable ground conditions. Beach worker Khairul Izdihar Ismail, 36, said the latest incident occurred about four days ago at 9pm during heavy rain and strong winds.

items.title: Johor police seek help to locate missing Rohingya girl, woman
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Johor police are seeking public assistance to locate two missing persons reported in separate cases in the state. A 14-year-old Rohingya girl, identified as Nurqayas Noor Mohamad, was reported missing by her father on March 30 from their home in Taman Nusantara, Iskandar Puteri. 8, Jalan Nusaria 3/4, Taman Nusantara, Gelang Patah.

items.title: Khaled Nordin: Desalination plant, cloud seeding deployed as water crisis hits 4,000 in Kota Tinggi
items.summary: KOTA TINGGI, April 22 — Authorities are deploying a mobile desalination plant and conducting cloud seeding operations to combat a severe water crisis affecting more than 4,000 residents in Felda Lok Heng and Felda Waha. According to Kota Tinggi MP Datuk Seri Khaled Nordin, prolonged dry weather has caused water levels at Sungai Ulu Sedili Kecil to drop to a critical point, rendering the Lok Heng Water Treatment Plant unable to operate. To address the crisis, water operator Ranhill SAJ is now relocating and installing a desalination plant from Forest City to the Lok Heng facility.

items.title: Malaysia’s strategic neutrality key to navigating US-China trade war, says Tengku Zafrul
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Malaysia should reform or recalibrate its response to changes in the global architecture, especially in trade, as they occur, said Malaysian Investment Development Authority (MIDA) chairman Tengku Datuk Seri Zafrul Abdul Aziz. He said the country should also remain mindful of its strategic positioning as a middle power, leveraging its strengths while addressing concerns about energy security, technology, and overall resilience to remain part of both the United States and China’s supply chain ecosystems. Tengku Zafrul further noted that while China remains Malaysia’s and ASEAN’s largest trading partner, the US is Malaysia’s largest investor and export market and possibly for the broader region as well.

items.title: May 13 hearing for Isa Samad’s bid to review prison term, RM15.45m fine
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Former Felda chairman Tan Sri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad’s application to review the Federal Court’s decision on his conviction and sentence will be heard on May 13. Lawyer Datuk Abu Bakar Isa Ramat, representing Mohd Isa, and Deputy Public Prosecutor Afzainizam Abdul Aziz confirmed the hearing date when contacted. Mohd Isa is currently serving his sentence at Sungai Buloh Prison.

items.title: Zakat Selangor says has absolutely no links to RM230m NGO fraud case
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 22 — Lembaga Zakat Selangor (Zakat Selangor) has stressed that it has no connection to the alleged misappropriation of zakat funds amounting to RM230 million, which is currently under investigation by the authorities. Zakat Selangor chief executive officer Mohd Khaidzir Shahari said the institution operates as Selangor’s official zakat authority under the supervision of the Selangor Islamic Religious Council (MAIS), and is governed by legal provisions as well as a comprehensive governance and regulatory framework with checks and balances. Mohd Khaidzir said Zakat Selangor maintained a firm stance against any form of misuse of zakat funds and would always ensure that zakat management was carried out in accordance with the established governance framework.

items.title: MACC clarifies that RM230m misappropriated was public donations, not ‘zakat’ funds
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has clarified that the alleged misappropriation of RM230 million by a non-governmental organisation (NGO) did not involve funds from any official zakat institution, but rather public donations intended for the needy. The clarification comes after two individuals, including the NGO’s deputy chairman, were remanded for four days to assist in the high-profile investigation. “In MACC's latest investigation, this NGO received donation funds from various parties and sources, including commissions obtained from certain collections, to be channelled to those in need,” he said.

items.title: Rain, breakdown and TikTok gifts: Malay mechanic and Chinese motorcyclist become symbol of cross-cultural kindness in Malaysia (VIDEO)
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — A chance roadside encounter due to a motorcycle breakdown and a TikTok livestream have sparked an unusual chain of kindness between a Chinese rider known as Dicky and a Malay mechanic called Abang Usop, drawing public attention and an emerging crowd-funded goodwill effort. Abang Usop said he completed the repair and realised the rider Dicky only had RM80, even after borrowing from a friend. Feeling that the rider was genuinely in need, Abang Usop said he used donation money to settle the matter instead, adding that he was moved by the encounter.

items.title: Sultan Ibrahim orders royal foundation to assist Sandakan families displaced by massive fire
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — His Majesty Sultan Ibrahim, King of Malaysia, has graciously extended a personal contribution to assist families affected by the fire tragedy in Kampung Bahagia, Sandakan. According to a Facebook post by Sultan Ibrahim Sultan Iskandar, His Majesty decreed that the contribution will be channelled through Yayasan Sultan Ibrahim Johor (YSIJ) to help ease the burden of families impacted by the incident. In this regard, Sultan Ibrahim has instructed the foundation (YSIJ) to liaise with the Sabah state government to ensure the assistance reaches the affected families.

items.title: Plans for three-star hotel in Kenyir Geopark hit snag, Terengganu govt seeks new push for tourism
items.summary: KUALA TERENGGANU, April 22 — Operations at the three‑star hotel on Pulau Poh in Kenyir Geopark near Hulu Terengganu, due to launch in March this year, have been postponed after the previous operator withdrew. State Tourism, Culture, Environment and Climate Change Committee chairman, Datuk Razali Idris, said the state government is meanwhile working to develop ancillary products around Pulau Poh. “Regarding the government hotel on Pulau Poh, the project is still within its defect liability period.

items.title: Facebook posts on 3R issues land two men in court; 64-year-old gets four-month jail, retired soldier fights charges
items.summary: SEPANG, April 22 — Two men faced court action over offensive social media posts, with one jailed after pleading guilty and another claiming trial on similar charges involving sensitive issues of race, religion and royalty (3R). In the first case, Mat Derus Ali, 64, was sentenced to four months’ jail by the Sessions Court here after admitting to uploading offensive content against the royal institution on Facebook in August last year. The post was later detected at the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC) Tower in Cyberjaya.

items.title: Penang CM says no WFH yet for state civil servants, priority is public service delivery
items.summary: GEORGE TOWN, April 22 — The Penang State Executive Council (MMK) has decided not to implement the work-from-home (WFH) policy for state civil servants for the time being. However, he said the state government will continue to monitor developments and review the need for the policy from time to time. On April 1, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the Cabinet had agreed to implement a WFH policy involving ministries, agencies, statutory bodies and government-linked companies (GLCs), effective April 15.

items.title: Kangar PKR hit by resignations amid attendance row
items.summary: KANGAR, April 22 — A total of 15 committee members from PKR’s Kangar division, including its deputy chief, have resigned after failing to attend multiple party meetings, local news outlets reported today. Kangar division chief Noor Amin Ahmad affirmed the resignations but rejected suggestions that the division is in turmoil, saying the departures came after show-cause letters were issued over repeated absences from official sessions, Free Malaysia Today reported. An earlier news report by Utusan Malaysia citing anonymous sources alleged that those who resigned had written to PKR’s central leadership claiming mismanagement and weak leadership in the Kangar division.

items.title: RM5b MSME aid only ‘small win,’ more needed to tackle crisis, says Guan Eng
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — DAP advisor Lim Guan Eng has described the government’s latest RM5 billion aid package for small businesses as a “small win,” urging Putrajaya to implement bolder measures like making the first RM50,000 of all loans interest-free and collateral-free. The Bagan MP was responding to Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim’s announcement of several measures to support micro, small and medium enterprises (MSMEs), including the new loan facility and a one-year delay for e-invoicing implementation. “I urge the prime minister to approve the first RM 50,000 portion of the entire loan be deemed interest-free and collateral free.

items.title: Ikram suspends three members caught in RM230m ‘zakat’ fund scandal
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Pertubuhan Ikram Malaysia (Ikram) has suspended three members arrested by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) in connection with a massive investigation into the alleged misappropriation of approximately RM230 million in zakat funds. The move comes after two individuals, including the deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation (NGO), were remanded for four days starting today to assist in the MACC probe. In a statement today, Ikram president Badlishah Sham Baharin said that the organisation views the matter seriously and is extending its full cooperation to the MACC.

items.title: RM230m zakat funds probe: Two suspects, including NGO deputy chairman, remanded for four days over alleged misappropriation
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 22 — Two individuals, including the deputy chairman of a non-governmental organisation (NGO), have been remanded for four days, from today, to assist in investigations into the alleged misappropriation of about RM230 million in zakat funds. Earlier, the two individuals arrived at the Shah Alam Court complex at 9.07am in a MACC vehicle. Yesterday, the Selangor MACC arrested three individuals, including a deputy chairman of an NGO, on suspicion of misappropriating about RM230 million in zakat funds.

items.title: Exco: Selangor considering seawater desalination for water security
items.summary: He said the construction of the infrastructure could increase clean water production capacity by about 300 million litres per day (MLD) for use in the state. Izham said Selangor’s current water reserve margin stands at around 17.4 per cent, or 1,130 MLD, while total clean water production capacity from all 34 water treatment plants (LRAs) is 6,500 MLD, with average actual demand at 5,370 MLD. He said Pengurusan Air Selangor Sdn Bhd (Air Selangor) also expects the required annual water reserve capacity to increase by about 20 per cent over the next five years.

items.title: Johor man charged with bribing police RM18,500 to ignore friends’ criminal histories
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, April 22 — A 31-year-old factory supervisor pleaded not guilty at the Sessions Court here today to a charge of bribing a police officer with RM18,500 to allegedly overlook his and his friends' criminal records. The accused, M. Dhivan, made the plea after the charge was read to him in Bahasa Melayu before Judge Datuk Che Wan Zaidi Che Wan Ibrahim. According to the charge sheet, Dhivan is accused of corruptly giving RM18,500 in cash to Sergeant Koshaziza Idris of the Segamat district police headquarters' patrol unit.

items.title: Federal Court dismisses challenge against whipping as cruel and inhumane
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, April 22 — In a 2-1 majority decision, the Federal Court today dismissed review applications by three prisoners seeking to set aside their whipping sentences, ruling that the punishment is constitutional. Chief Justice Datuk Seri Wan Ahmad Farid Wan Salleh and Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi formed the majority, while Federal Court judge Datuk Lee Swee Seng delivered the dissenting decision. In delivering the majority decision, Justice Wan Ahmad said whipping did not fall within the ambit of a “cruel, oppressive, and degrading” punishment in the constitutional sense.

items.title: Mother pleads for hit-and-run Avanza driver to come forward after son loses leg in Kuching
items.summary: KUCHING, April 22 — A mother is appealing for the driver involved in a hit-and-run incident in late March that left her only son with his left leg amputated to come forward and lodge a police report to assist with investigations. Afidah Suhaili, 68, said her family harbours no resentment and only hopes the individual will take responsibility for the incident. She also urged anyone with CCTV or dashcam footage of the incident to assist with police investigations.

items.title: Selangor accelerates greening campaign after planting eight million trees since 2022
items.summary: SHAH ALAM, April 22 — The Selangor government is on track to achieve its goal of planting 11 million trees, having already surpassed eight million since its state-wide greening campaign began, the state assembly was told today. State Public Health and Environment Committee chairman Jamaliah Jamaluddin confirmed that the initiative, launched in May 2022, has recorded a total of 8,050,011 trees planted across the state. New target set for 2026 To maintain momentum, Jamaliah announced a new target for 2026, calling on each local authority and government agency to plant approximately 100,000 trees throughout the year.

items.title: Actor Ako Mustapha alleges son bullied by five students; police confirm report
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 2 — Police have confirmed receiving a report regarding the alleged bullying of actor Ako Mustapha’s 10-year-old son, Mikael Iskandar, by five other students at a school here. Wangsa Maju District Police Chief Assistant Commissioner Annas Sulaiman verified that his agency has been notified, Berita Harian reported. “I confirm that the Wangsa Maju District Police Headquarters (IPD) has received a police report as stated.

items.title: Sandakan fire witness says sorry after video of firefighter abuse goes viral
items.summary: SANDAKAN, April 22 — A man has apologised to the Fire and Rescue Department for using abusive language in a viral video recorded during the recent fire at Kampung Bahagia here. Rozaizar Rozainie, 39, said his actions were driven by heightened emotions and stress as he witnessed the chaotic scene, including children panicking as the flames spread. The Fire and Rescue Department had earlier said firefighting operations faced several challenges, including narrow access routes, the absence of fire hydrants, and strong winds that accelerated the spread of the fire.

items.title: Ahmad Zahid: Umno’s 80 years more than just a number, it’s a legacy
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, April 22 — Umno president Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Zahid Hamidi has described the party as an enduring platform of struggle, ahead of its 80th anniversary on May 11. The Deputy Prime Minister said that since its inception, Umno has embarked on a long journey that has shaped the country’s direction, from independence to development, through sacrifice, leadership and relentless effort. Ahmad Zahid said Umno, founded in 1946 as a movement to oppose the Malayan Union and defend the country’s sovereignty, was born out of the spirit of unity and courage of the people.

items.title: Sipitang residents warned after crocodile seen near water treatment plant
items.summary: He said the crocodile was seen swimming in the middle of the river. The sighting raised concerns among residents, particularly those who frequent the river for daily activities. Recent reports also highlighted crocodile sightings in coastal waters in Labuan, raising broader safety concerns among communities.
